content.full_text:
California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
Lori Crawford 
Accounting Assistant 
December 26, 1989 
California Cable Television Association 
4341 Piedmont Avenue 
Oakland, CA 94611 
Dear Ms. Crawford: 
Re: 
Your Request for Advice 
Our File No. A-89-679 
You have requested confirmation of telephone advice provided 
to you concerning the campaign provisions of the Political Reform 
Act. 
(Government Code Sections 81000-91015.) 
Your letter, dated November 22, 1989, correctly summarizes my 
advice 
Your payment of $500 to Gwen Moore for presenting an award 
at a Cable Television Association function would not be considered 
a campaign contribution. 
You have indicated to me that this was 
not arranged by the Association's lobbyist, so the payment would 
not be subject to the gift restrictions on lobbyists. 
In 
addition, since this is not a payment for a speech on the 
governmental process, it is not subject to the $1,000 limitation 
on gifts and honoraria. 
A copy of your letter is enclosed for your convenience. 
Please contact me at (916) 322-5662 if you have any 
additional questions. 
Enclosure 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel

Political Reform Consultant 
428} Street, Suite 800 • 
P.O. Box 807 • Sacramento CA. 958(~4~0807 • 
(916) 322~5660 


November 22, 1989 
Hr. Wayne Imberi 
Fair Political Practices Commission 
428 J Street, Suite 800 
Sacramento, CA 
95804 
Dear Hr. Imberi: 
Last month we talked about a contribution that was given to Gwen 
Hoore of $500 for presenting an award at a cable function. 
It 
was discussed whether this should be a political contribution, as 
she would simply be presenting this award and would not be 
discussing the legislative process in any way. 
After you reviewed the rules and consulted with a lawyer, it was 
determined that this would not be a political contribution and 
would not be in the $2500 legislative limits. 
Would you please verify this send and send back an endorsement 
stating that this would not be considered a political 
contribution. 
As we are audited on a yearly basis, this information would be 
added to campaign information files for any future campaign 
audits. 
Thank you for your help in this matter. 
Please give me a call if 
you should have any further questions. 
I have enclosed a return envelope with postage for your 
convenience. 
Sincerely, 
Lori Crawford 
Accounting Assistant 
Ilec 
Enclosure 


California 
Fair Political 
Practices Commission 
November ]0, 1989 
Lori Crawford 
Accounting Assistant 
California Cable Television 
Association 
P.O. Box 11080 
Oakland, CA 
94611 
Dear Ms. Crawford: 
Re: 
Letter No. 89-6'19 
We received your letter requesting confirmation 01 
advic~ 
under the Political Reform Act on November 27, 1989. 
Your letter 
has been assigned to our Technical Assistance and Analysis 
Division for response. 
If you have any questions, you may contact 
that division directly at (916) ]22-5662. 
If the letter is appropriate for confirmation without turther 
analysis, we will attempt to expedite our response. 
A confirming 
response will be released after it has gone through our approval 
process. 
If the letter is not appropriate for this treatment, the 
staff person assigned to prepare the response will contact you 
shortly to advise you. 
In such cases, the normal analysis, reVlew 
and approval process will be followed. 
You should be aware that your letter and our response are 
public records which may be disclosed to any interested person 
upon receipt of a proper request for disclosure. 
KED:plh:confadv1 
Sincerely, 
Kathryn E. Donovan 
General Counsel

In addition, since this is not a payment for a speech on the governmental process, it is not subject to the $1,000 limitation on gifts and honoraria.

sections.question_synthetic: The requestor asked whether a $500 payment to an individual for presenting an award at a cable television association function would constitute a campaign contribution or be subject to gift limitations under the Political Reform Act.
sections.conclusion_synthetic: The FPPC concluded that the $500 payment to Gwen Moore would not constitute a campaign contribution, would not be subject to lobbyist gift restrictions, and would not be subject to the $1,000 limitation on gifts and honoraria since it did not involve a speech on the governmental process.
sections.parsing_notes: Format: ocr; LLM: Clear FPPC advice letter with formal Q&A structure. Includes both the incoming request letter and FPPC's confirming response letter. File number A-89-679. Some OCR artifacts present but core content is clear.

embedding.qa_text:
QUESTION: Would a $500 payment to Gwen Moore for presenting an award at a Cable Television Association function be considered a campaign contribution subject to political reform restrictions?

CONCLUSION: Your payment of $500 to Gwen Moore for presenting an award at a Cable Television Association function would not be considered a campaign contribution. You have indicated to me that this was not arranged by the Association's lobbyist, so the payment would not be subject to the gift restrictions on lobbyists. In addition, since this is not a payment for a speech on the governmental process, it is not subject to the $1,000 limitation on gifts and honoraria.

embedding.summary: The FPPC confirmed previous telephone advice to the California Cable Television Association regarding a $500 payment to an individual for presenting an award, determining it was not a campaign contribution and not subject to applicable gift or honoraria limitations under the Political Reform Act.
